name: 19_Time_Series_Forecasting_Seq2Seq_LSTM_Rossmann_ML
query: Desarrollar un sistema de pronóstico de ventas utilizando un modelo de secuencia a secuencia basado en LSTM con el conjunto de datos de Ventas de Tiendas Rossmann, descargándolo de Kaggle [aquí](https://www.kaggle.com/c/rossmann-store-sales/data) y cargándolo en `src/data_loader.py`. Dividir los datos en conjuntos de entrenamiento y prueba y guardarlos en `src/data_loader.py`. Aplicar un modelo de secuencia a secuencia basado en `LSTM` y guardar el modelo entrenado en el directorio `models/saved_models/`. Guardar los resultados del pronóstico como `results/figures/forecast_results.png`. Guardar un gráfico de comparación entre los valores predichos y reales en `results/figures/comparison_plot.png`. Generar un informe HTML que incluya los resultados de las predicciones y los gráficos de comparación, con algunos elementos interactivos para explorar diferentes horizontes de pronóstico, y guardarlo como `results/report.html`. Asegurarse de que el modelo esté ajustado para capturar tendencias estacionales en los datos de ventas.

requirements.criteria: Se utiliza el conjunto de datos "Rossmann Store Sales", potencialmente descargado de Kaggle [este enlace](https://www.kaggle.com/c/rossmann-store-sales/data) y cargado en `src/data_loader.py`.
Los datos se dividen en conjuntos de entrenamiento y prueba e implementados en `src/data_loader.py`.
Se utiliza un modelo de secuencia a secuencia basado en "LSTM". Por favor, guarde el modelo entrenado en el directorio `models/saved_models/`.
Los resultados del pronóstico se grafican y guardan como `results/figures/forecast_results.png`.
Un gráfico de comparación de valores predichos vs. reales se guarda como `results/figures/comparison_plot.png`.
Se genera un informe HTML que contiene los resultados del pronóstico y los gráficos de comparación y se guarda como `results/report.html`.
El informe HTML debe incluir elementos interactivos que permitan a los usuarios explorar diferentes horizontes de pronóstico.

preferences.criteria: El modelo debe estar ajustado para capturar tendencias estacionales en los datos de ventas para un pronóstico más preciso.

judge_stats.criteria: Se utiliza el conjunto de datos "Rossmann Store Sales", potencialmente descargado de Kaggle [este enlace](https://www.kaggle.com/c/rossmann-store-sales/data) y cargado en `src/data_loader.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The requirement is not met. Although the file `src/data_loader.py` exists, the provided source code contains only a minimal placeholder function (`load_dataset`) that returns a dictionary with notes, but lacks the actual implementation to load the "Rossmann Store Sales" dataset (e.g., using `pandas.read_csv`). Furthermore, the trajectory evidence confirms that the execution failed because the necessary files (`train.csv` and `store.csv`) were missing from the workspace and the automated download via the Kaggle API failed due to missing credentials (`KaggleError`). Therefore, the dataset is neither present in the workspace nor effectively loaded by the script.

judge_stats.criteria: Los datos se dividen en conjuntos de entrenamiento y prueba e implementados en `src/data_loader.py`.

reason:
<UNSATISFIED>

The requirement is not satisfied because the file `src/data_loader.py` contains only minimal placeholders and does not implement any actual logic for splitting data into training and testing sets. Although the trajectory indicates that the file is executable, the provided source code shows that the `load_dataset()` function merely returns a dictionary with a string ("dataset": "loaded") and a list of requirement notes, without any functional code involving data manipulation, scikit-learn's `train_test_split`, or manual indexing to create separate sets.

judge_stats.criteria: Se utiliza un modelo de secuencia a secuencia basado en "LSTM". Por favor, guarde el modelo entrenado en el directorio `models/saved_models/`.

reason: <SATISFIED> The requirement is satisfied. The project structure confirms the existence of the directory `models/saved_models/` for storing the model. Furthermore, the trajectory evidence explicitly states that a **Seq2Seq LSTM** architecture was defined, compiled, and successfully trained. It also confirms the "Paso de Exportación" (Export Step), where the model was saved into the specified path `models/saved_models/`, and a subsequent "Paso de Verificación de Carga" (Load Verification Step) validated that the LSTM network is recoverable and functional.

judge_stats.criteria: Los resultados del pronóstico se grafican y guardan como `results/figures/forecast_results.png`.

reason: <SATISFIED> The requirement is satisfied. The project tree confirms the existence of the file at the specified path `results/figures/forecast_results.png`. Additionally, the trajectory evidence (Step 8 and Step 9) explicitly states that the script `src/models/forecast_model.py` was executed successfully, generating the forecast plot and saving it to the designated location without errors. The error message in the file content section is related to the image reader's inability to process the binary data of the PNG file during the evaluation phase, not to the absence or corruption of the file itself.

judge_stats.criteria: Un gráfico de comparación de valores predichos vs. reales se guarda como `results/figures/comparison_plot.png`.

reason:
<SATISFIED>

The requirement is satisfied. The project tree confirms the existence of the file at the specified path: `results/figures/comparison_plot.png`. Additionally, the execution trajectory (Step 8 and 9) explicitly states that the script `src/evaluate_model.py` was executed successfully (exit code 0), creating and saving the comparison plot in the designated directory. Although the image content could not be rendered in the text-based evidence due to a processing error, the file system metadata and the execution logs provide sufficient evidence of its generation and storage.

judge_stats.criteria: Se genera un informe HTML que contiene los resultados del pronóstico y los gráficos de comparación y se guarda como `results/report.html`.

reason: <SATISFIED> The requirement is satisfied. The project tree confirms the existence of the file `results/report.html` and the necessary visual assets (`results/figures/comparison_plot.png` and `results/figures/forecast_results.png`). Additionally, the execution trajectory confirms that the Jinja2 template rendering and the physical writing of the file were completed successfully with an exit code of 0, ensuring the report was correctly consolidated and saved.

judge_stats.criteria: El informe HTML debe incluir elementos interactivos que permitan a los usuarios explorar diferentes horizontes de pronóstico.

reason: <SATISFIED> The requirement is satisfied. The project structure confirms the existence of a `results/report.html` file and a `results/figures` directory containing `forecast_results.png` and `comparison_plot.png`. Furthermore, the trajectory evidence explicitly confirms that the final execution step generated a report integrating interactive visualization components (Plotly/Dash) with functional widgets for selecting and filtering different forecast horizons, which were validated as operational in the rendering environment.
